evidence.role: Former employee - IT Risk and Assurance Staff
evidence.text: Opportunities to travel domestically and internationally, lots of smart people, top technology, above average salaries, lots of opportunities to learn Good and Bad about ExxonMobil
Stable job and pension compensation Toxic Company Culture
Good growth opportunities and decent pay Integrity engineer

Long hours long hours long hours Cool
Low pay for hourly workers. Too top heavy Low pay

Long hours (expected to work weekends if needed) Performance ranking system Must play politics to advance in your career Great company to learn and advance your career, but expect long hours and politics
Have to work 65 hours a week because I was on salary and they wanted to work me to death Overworked and underpaid
Supervisors are narcissists, toxic gang like behaviors stay away. Dont get tricked by the name of the company TOXIC

If you're a creative professional looking for a supportive and collaborative workplace, look elsewhere. This company fosters a fear-based work culture through its toxic ranking system, which pits employees against one another, including against their own managers. Thereâs a reason why performance assessments are jokingly referred to as âThe Hunger Gamesâ among employees. Leadership thrives on ambiguity, and ma...
New Mexico group is toxic Toxic environment despite decent pay
Too many. Too much politics and a complete lack of meritocracy. Rigged performance review. No career path for technical folks. Toxic company
Spend more time marketing yourself than working Cut throat competition Unfair ranking system Toxic company with too many standards
No work life balance always on calls. No work life balance
Absolutely no work life balance. I live 72 miles from campus and have to come in EVERYDAY. I spend all my time on calls or in my car in traffic. No work life balance

Everybody in my group quit (a trend these days), so my hours have been closer to 12+ hr days. Honestly I'm just not passionate about oil and gas. The pay can't be beat if you're a civil engineer, which is why I decided to give the job a chance. I'm actively looking for jobs elsewhere now because I can't scratch the itch for wanting to work on a civil infrastructure or building construction project (the reason I chos...
Did not value employees work life balance Not good work life balance

evidence.role: Former employee - Global Supply Chain Advisor
evidence.text: Toxic culture, poor resource management, heavy workload, long hours, no work life balance Culture needs attention

Were not transparent with employees about layoffs (percentages and reasoning) which made a lot of people feel on edge all of the time Long working hours and always seemed to run too lean staffing wise. Whenever I left a project or a functional team, I was replaced with 2 people. Great Colleagues but Long Hours
Highly Competitive, Cut Throat, Arrogant, Not Inclusive Toxic Work Environment

Senior management is rigid and very cost conscious. These 2 years extreme cost cutting even though we are making good profits. Place is very bureaucratic and very compartmentalized... have to talk to many functions to push through a simple project. Benefits is quite poor. Only salary is competitive when u become more senior Place for Long term Career but rigid manaagement
Incredibly rigid culture, ranking always a suprise Rigid
Rigid promotion structure, delayed compensation structure Great company, rigid promotion structure - Long term compensation structure
ExxonMobil prefers to do business with established practices. They will typically not be at the leading edge. Stable with opportunity for growth
Stagnant growth and biased against older employees Limited upward mobility

While there was room for growth and promotion was present, the compensation did not match up specifically for my own role. I did not remain with the company long enough to witness if this would change. Please keep in mind that this may differ based on whatever area of the business that your role reports to. Incredible Experience
Theres not a whole lot of growth n/a
No career, no transparency, forced remote work from an office location, training your replacements Projects group is a dead-end
